text: The surgeon injects saline mixed with methylene blue to elevate the lesion, ensuring a clear margin for resection. A snare is positioned two to three millimeters below the polyp, and the Endocut Q-three-one-three setting is used for resection. Post-resection examination reveals no bleeding or perforation at the base.
The surgeon applies three clips to the base of the resection site, starting at the top end and moving downward, to prevent delayed bleeding. A tattoo from previous marking is visible at the base.
